fyMatch: FY 2025 federal baseline: 40% non-federal cost share unless state-specific guidance says otherwise.
summary: No clearly dated 2026 statewide subrecipient window was loaded into this starter state record. Monitor the state SAA, homeland security, emergency management, technology, or cybersecurity program page.
timeline: Watch for state SAA announcements, local application notices, webinar recordings, and state cybersecurity planning committee updates.
requirements: K-12 public school districts are generally prospect targets when treated as local government agencies or instrumentalities under state law. Validate state-specific eligibility before outreach.
accessPath: Identify the state administrative agency and watch for application portal, email submission, RFP/RFA, or state-provided services-in-lieu process.
targetingNote: Prioritize rural and remote public school districts first, then counties, municipalities, councils of government, public authorities, and public higher education.
immediateNextSteps: Confirm state SAA program page and registration path.
Sort K-12 prospects by Tier 1/Tier 2 rurality and district size.
Identify IT Director, Superintendent, Finance/Grants, and procurement contacts.
Track notes in the Prospect Activity log.

fyMatch: FY 2025 federal baseline: 40% non-federal cost share unless state-specific guidance says otherwise.
summary: Missouri DPS NOFO lists an April 13, 2026 start date, May 20, 2026 5:00 p.m. CST application deadline, and WebGrants submission path.
timeline: Application Start Date: April 13, 2026. Deadline: May 20, 2026, 5:00 p.m. CST.
requirements: Missouri requires forms and named attachments such as audit/financial statement, quote/cost basis, and CISA CPG report, and limits applicant agencies to one project/objective per application with up to four applications not to exceed $200,000 cumulatively.
accessPath: Missouri DPS online WebGrants System.
targetingNote: Target public districts and local governments with defined cyber projects and quote-ready scope.
immediateNextSteps: Confirm state SAA program page and registration path.
Sort K-12 prospects by Tier 1/Tier 2 rurality and district size.
Identify IT Director, Superintendent, Finance/Grants, and procurement contacts.

summary: Pennsylvania says local governments cannot apply directly to the federal government, eligible entities will be contacted when applications open, and more information to apply for 2025 funding will be provided later.
timeline: Date TBD; sign up for updates and monitor PEMA page.
requirements: Pennsylvania services include asset intelligence, intrusion detection, security awareness services, and SIEM.
accessPath: Commonwealth will reach out to eligible entities when applications open.
targetingNote: Use the school district note on Pennsylvania page: past positive feedback led to expansion requests to school districts and municipal/city governments.

fyMatch: FY 2025 federal baseline: 40% non-federal cost share unless state-specific guidance says otherwise.
summary: Montana says the most recent window closed January 9, 2026 and the next application window will open by late spring 2026.
timeline: Most recent due date: January 9, 2026. Next window: late spring 2026.
requirements: Montana lists local governments as eligible and specifically includes school districts; nonprofit and for-profit entities are not eligible. Focus areas include security awareness training, technical workforce professional development, EDR, network traffic monitoring, and MFA.
accessPath: Montana DES application process; submit completed application to MTDESGrants@mt.gov when window opens.
targetingNote: Strong K-12 fit due to school district eligibility and defined cyber service focus areas.
immediateNextSteps: Confirm state SAA program page and registration path.
